Assumption Statement: True support size is sparse relative to ambient dimension
Transferability Caveat: Need extension from pairwise precision/operator sparsity to k>=3 hyperedge operators in switching dynamics

Assumption Statement: Regime-conditioned emissions/operators are separated by a positive margin and transitions are nondegenerate
Closest Literature Archetype: Identifiability of HMM/SDS with distinct emissions and ergodic transitions
Transferability Caveat: Exact separation metric for high-order tensor emissions must be formalized in our theorem

Assumption Statement: Noise is sub-Gaussian (with robust extension path for heavy tails)
Closest Literature Archetype: Concentration-driven sparse estimation in dependent high-dimensional models
Transferability Caveat: Need dependence-aware concentration for latent-switching high-order observables

Assumption Statement: Design/operator geometry satisfies incoherence/irrepresentable or RE-type condition
Transferability Caveat: Need explicit hyperedge-tensor analog and verification statistic

Assumption Statement: Latent regime process mixes sufficiently fast for effective sample size concentration
Closest Literature Archetype: Markov-chain concentration and HMM identifiability under ergodicity
Transferability Caveat: Need explicit translation from spectral gap/mixing time to support-recovery sample complexity in our model

Assumption Statement: Nonzero parameters exceed a beta-min signal floor tied to sample size/noise
Closest Literature Archetype: Exact support recovery thresholds in sparse estimation
Validity Verdict: Strongly supported requirement for exact support claims
Transferability Caveat: Need beta-min formulation for multilinear hyperedge parameters under latent switching
